violations.description: Preventing contamination during storage. — --Food item was not properly protected from potential contamination. -Hot hold display is below heaviliy soiled wire shelf.--Food item was not stored in a clean, dry location a minimum of 6 inches above the floor. -3 cases of food stored on floor in walk in freezer.

Refrigerated Storage. — --Refrigerator or cold storage was insufficient or ineffective at maintaining potentially hazardous food at a temperature of 41F or less. -Small prep table near grill found with temps of 45-47F in bottom of unit.

Cooling of potentially hazardous food. — --Cooked potentially hazardous food was not cooled properly from 70F to 41F or below within four hours. -Vodka sauce made yesterday (near walk in cooler glass doors) with center temperature of 47F and edge temperature of 45F and with heavy condensation at lid.

Cleaning frequency of equipment and utensils. — --Nonfood contact surface was not cleaned as frequently as required. -Debris/residue at wire shelved in walk in cooler. -Debris/residue at wire shelf above hot hold unit. -Worn/dirty gaskets at prep table doors. -Dust/debris at walk in freezer fans.--Ventilation hood system was not cleaned properly and/or as frequently as required. -Grease/debris build up at hood vents and fire suppression lines above fryers.
Design and construction of floors, walls, and ceilings. — --Soda closet is missing an outlet and switch cover and has an uncovered junctions box near soda bibs.

Cleaning and maintenance of floors, walls and ceilings. — --Physical facilities (e.g. floors, walls, ceiling etc…) were not maintained in good repair. -Floors throughout kitchen are worn/unsealed cement or damaged tile. -Side wall is unsealed/raw wood at ice scoop for use at server soda station. -Walk in cooler shelves have worn/ peeling paint. -Raw worn wood shelving in walk in freezer.

Handling and storage of poisonous and toxic materials. — --Secondary container of poisonous or toxic material was not clearly identified to indicated contents. -Spray bottle at bar was unlabeled and had unknown chemical in bottle.

General food protection. — ---Staff did not follow thawing instructions for raw vacuum sealed fish portions.--Food item was not protected from potential contamination. -Boxes of sausage were left open below fans in of walk in freezer/not protected from ice/frost.

Preventing contamination from equipment, utensils, and wiping cloths. — --In-use utensil was not properly stored during pauses in food preparation or dispensing. -Ice scoop at bar is stored in tray with bottles of liquor. -Ice scoop at server soda dispenser is stored in contact with rough wall surface.

Preventing contamination by consumers. — --Utensil was not suitable or dispensed effectively for self-service operations of ready-to-eat foods. -Portion cup left in/used as a scoop in Cajun sauce below prep table.

Cleaning frequency of equipment and utensils. — --Equipment that dispenses liquid/food/ice in unpackaged form was not cleaned properly and/or as frequently as required. -Limescale build up at interior panel edges and screws of ice machine.--Food contact surface was not cleaned as frequently as required. -Food debris at can opener balde and surface. -Water build up in bottom of small prep table at cookline.

Design and construction of floors, walls, and ceilings. — --Floors, walls, and/or ceiling were not constructed or designed to be smooth, nonabsorbent, and easily cleanable. -Open ceiling on left side of vent hood.

Cleaning and maintenance of floors, walls and ceilings. — --Physical facilities (e.g. floors, walls, ceiling etc. …) were not maintained in good repair. -Stained ceiling throughout kitchen area. -Floors throughout kitchen are worn/unsealed cement or damaged tile. -Walk in freezer has worn wood shelves and ice build up at floor below fans.
Design, construction, clean, maintained (1 pt deducted)
Prevented, separated, segregated (5 pts deducted)
